---
title: "vec2sym"
generated: true
description: "Symmetric matrix from the vec of its upper triangle."
---

[‹ back to `nitrix.linalg.matrix`](../nitrix-linalg-matrix.qmd)

*decorated:* `@partial(jax.custom_vjp, nondiff_argnums=(1,))`

<!-- nitrix:gen sig:vec2sym -->
```python
vec2sym(vec: Num[Array, '... n'], offset: int = 1) -> Num[Array, '... i i']
```
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:vec2sym -->
Symmetric matrix from the vec of its upper triangle.

The inverse of [`sym2vec`](../nitrix-linalg-matrix/sym2vec.qmd).  The matrix side length is recovered
from `n` in closed form as
$\lfloor (\sqrt{8 n + 1} + 1) / 2 \rfloor + (\text{offset} - 1)$,
then the vector is written into the upper triangle at the named
offset and reflected across it: entries off the named diagonal are
duplicated into the lower triangle, while entries on it are left
unduplicated.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen params:vec2sym -->
**Parameters**

| parameter | type | shape | default | description |
|------------|--------|--------|----------|--------------------------------------------------------------|
| `vec` | `Num` | `...` × `n` | — | Vectorised upper triangle, with arbitrary leading batch axes and a trailing axis of length `n`. |
| `offset` | `int` | — | `1` | Diagonal offset at which `vec` was extracted. Default `1` (the strict upper triangle, excluding the main diagonal). Use `offset=0` if `vec` includes the main diagonal. |

**Returns** `Num` — shape `...` × `i` × `i`. The reconstructed symmetric matrix block, carrying the leading batch axes of `vec`.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->


<!-- nitrix:gen dims:vec2sym -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
| `i` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->
